hypothesis: If pre-trained machine learning interatomic potentials (EquiformerV2 and eSEN) are used to generate converged initial pathway guesses for solid-state nudged elastic band calculations before DFT refinement, then the hybrid ML-SSNEB framework will significantly reduce the total number of computationally expensive DFT SSNEB iterations required for convergence compared to pure DFT-only SSNEB, because the ML-derived minimum energy pathways provide geometrically superior starting configurations that are closer to the true DFT minimum energy pathway, thereby accelerating convergence without sacrificing accuracy.
